name: Transformer Encoder-Decoder
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG (no external files, no scripts, no images) that reproduces the canonical Transformer encoder-decoder architecture figure. Title 'Transformer (Encoder-Decoder)' at top in 32px bold. Two parallel vertical stacks side by side. LEFT 'Encoder' stack wrapped in a labelled 'Nx' container, bottom to top: 'Input Embedding', '+ Positional Encoding', 'Multi-Head Self-Attention', 'Add & Norm', 'Feed Forward', 'Add & Norm'. RIGHT 'Decoder' stack wrapped in a labelled 'Nx' container, bottom to top: 'Output Embedding (shifted right)', '+ Positional Encoding', 'Masked Multi-Head Self-Attention', 'Add & Norm', 'Encoder-Decoder Cross-Attention', 'Add & Norm', 'Feed Forward', 'Add & Norm'. Above the decoder: 'Linear', 'Softmax', 'Output Probabilities'. From the top of the encoder stack draw TWO labelled arrows 'Keys (K)' and 'Values (V)' routing rightward into the decoder's 'Encoder-Decoder Cross-Attention' box. Draw residual skip arcs on the OUTER side of each stack: one per 'Add & Norm' (2 on the encoder left, 3 on the decoder right). Each box is a labelled rounded rectangle; the two stacks share a baseline grid so corresponding rows align. The 'Nx' multiplier label appears once on each stack container. Use dimension annotation 'd_model = 512' near the embeddings. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.
requirements: All boxes (6 encoder sublayers, 8 decoder sublayers, Linear, Softmax, Output Probabilities, 2 Nx containers) are visible within the 1920x1080 viewport with no clipping at any edge
No two boxes overlap each other
Every box label text is fully contained inside its rectangle with no text overflowing the box edges
Both 'Nx' multiplier labels are visible, one per stack container, and do not overlap any box
The 'Keys (K)' and 'Values (V)' cross-attention arrows run from the encoder top into the decoder 'Encoder-Decoder Cross-Attention' box and neither passes through any other box
The 'Keys (K)' and 'Values (V)' arrow labels do not overlap each other or any box
Residual skip arcs (2 on encoder left, 3 on decoder right) do not pass through any box and do not overlap each other
Encoder sublayer boxes have equal vertical gaps, and decoder sublayer boxes have equal vertical gaps (even spacing within each stack)
The 'd_model = 512' annotation is legible and does not overlap any box or other text
No element is within 10px of any viewport edge
No horizontal or vertical scrollbar appears; all content fits inside 1920x1080

A legend box maps every block color and arrow/line style used; each major block carries a tensor-shape annotation; sibling blocks are equal-size and edge-aligned with uniform gutters; no element overlaps or is clipped

name: ResNet Bottleneck Residual Block
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces a ResNet bottleneck residual block figure. Title 'ResNet Bottleneck Block' at top in 32px bold. A vertical main path of three convolution boxes in sequence, bottom to top: '1x1 conv, 64' (reduce), '3x3 conv, 64', '1x1 conv, 256' (expand), each followed by 'BN + ReLU' annotations. Below the stack an input tensor annotation 'x: 256-d', above the stack the output. To the RIGHT of the main path draw a curved identity skip-connection arrow that bypasses all three conv boxes and rejoins at a circular '+' (element-wise addition) node above the last conv, followed by a final 'ReLU' box. Label the skip arrow 'identity'. Show channel-count annotations (64, 64, 256) on each conv box and the bottleneck reduction ratio. The skip path must NOT pass through any conv box. Center the whole block. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.

name: GAN Generator + Discriminator
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces a GAN training figure with both networks. Title 'Generative Adversarial Network' at top in 32px bold. LEFT: a latent noise vector box 'z ~ N(0,1), 100-d' feeding into a 'Generator G' box (drawn as an upsampling trapezoid or stacked deconv boxes), producing a 'Fake Sample x_fake' box. A separate 'Real Sample x_real' box sits below/parallel. BOTH x_fake and x_real feed RIGHTWARD into a single 'Discriminator D' box (drawn as a downsampling trapezoid), which outputs a 'Real / Fake' probability box. Draw a labelled feedback arrow 'gradient / adversarial loss' curving from the Discriminator output BACK to the Generator. Annotate the two loss terms: 'D maximizes log D(x)' and 'G minimizes log(1 - D(G(z)))'. The real-sample arrow and the fake-sample arrow must both reach the Discriminator without crossing each other through any box. Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.

name: Feature Pyramid Network (FPN)
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces a Feature Pyramid Network figure. Title 'Feature Pyramid Network (FPN)' at top in 32px bold. LEFT column: a bottom-up backbone pathway with FOUR feature-map boxes stacked vertically, drawn at DECREASING spatial size top-to-bottom toward higher levels, labelled 'C2 (1/4)', 'C3 (1/8)', 'C4 (1/16)', 'C5 (1/32)' with channel annotations. RIGHT column: a top-down pathway with FOUR corresponding output boxes 'P5', 'P4', 'P3', 'P2', each at the same vertical level as its backbone counterpart. For each level draw a horizontal '1x1 conv (lateral connection)' arrow from Ck to the merge point. From each higher Pk draw a '2x upsample' downward arrow that merges (element-wise '+' node) with the lateral connection to form the lower P-level. Each Pk is followed by a '3x3 conv' box producing the final pyramid feature. Annotate '256-d' on all P-level outputs. Four levels, four lateral connections, three upsample-merge arrows. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.

name: Latent Diffusion U-Net with Timestep + Text Cross-Attention
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces a Stable-Diffusion / Latent-Diffusion denoising U-Net figure. Title 'Latent Diffusion U-Net (with conditioning)' at top in 32px bold. Draw a U-shaped network: a LEFT descending encoder of FOUR down-blocks at decreasing spatial resolution (annotate resolutions 64x64, 32x32, 16x16, 8x8 and channel counts), a bottleneck 'mid-block' at the bottom, and a RIGHT ascending decoder of FOUR up-blocks mirroring the encoder. Draw FOUR horizontal skip connections from each encoder level to its mirror decoder level. INPUT (left) is a 'Noisy Latent z_t (4 x 64 x 64)' box; OUTPUT (right) is 'Predicted Noise ε_θ'. Two conditioning signals enter: (1) a 'Timestep t -> sinusoidal embedding -> MLP' box whose embedding is injected into EVERY block (draw labelled injection arrows into each down/up block), and (2) a 'Text Prompt -> CLIP Text Encoder' box feeding 'cross-attention' into the blocks (draw cross-attention arrows into at least the mid and adjacent blocks). Annotate which blocks contain 'ResBlock' vs 'ResBlock + Cross-Attn'. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.

name: CLIP Dual-Encoder Contrastive Matrix
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces the CLIP contrastive pre-training figure. Title 'CLIP: Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training' at top in 32px bold. TOP-LEFT: a 'Text Encoder' (Transformer) box taking a column of N text inputs and producing N text embeddings T1..TN drawn as a horizontal row of small vectors across the TOP. LEFT side: an 'Image Encoder' (ResNet/ViT) box taking N images and producing N image embeddings I1..IN drawn as a vertical column of small vectors down the LEFT. In the center draw an N x N similarity matrix grid (use N = 4 or 5) where cell (i,j) = Ii · Tj; the DIAGONAL cells (matched pairs) are highlighted/shaded differently from off-diagonal cells. Label the contrastive objective 'maximize diagonal, minimize off-diagonal (symmetric cross-entropy)'. The text-embedding row must align column-wise with the matrix columns, and the image-embedding column must align row-wise with the matrix rows. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.

name: Mixture-of-Experts Layer
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) layer figure. Title 'Mixture-of-Experts Layer (Top-2 Gating)' at top in 32px bold. Bottom: an 'Input Token x' box. The token feeds into a 'Gating Network / Router (softmax)' box that outputs gating weights. Draw a horizontal row of EXACTLY SIX 'Expert' FFN boxes ('Expert 1' .. 'Expert 6') above the router. The router dispatches the token to the TOP-2 selected experts (draw SOLID arrows to exactly two highlighted experts, e.g. Expert 2 and Expert 5, and faded/dashed arrows or no arrows to the unselected four). Each selected expert's output multiplies by its gate weight 'g_i' and the selected outputs feed into a 'Weighted Combine (Σ g_i · E_i(x))' box at the top producing the output. Annotate the two gate weights (e.g. g_2 = 0.7, g_5 = 0.3). Six experts, top-2 routing. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.

name: Graph Attention Network Message-Passing Step
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces a Graph Attention Network (GAT) message-passing / aggregation step figure. Title 'Graph Attention Network: Node Update' at top in 32px bold. CENTER: a target node 'h_1' (circle). Around it draw FIVE neighbour nodes 'h_2', 'h_3', 'h_4', 'h_5', 'h_6' (circles) connected to h_1 by edges. On EACH edge from neighbour j to h_1 draw a labelled attention coefficient 'α_1j' (α_12 .. α_16), with the edges drawn at different thicknesses/opacities to indicate different attention weights. To the SIDE, draw an inset showing the attention computation: 'e_1j = LeakyReLU(aᵀ[W h_1 || W h_j])' then 'α_1j = softmax_j(e_1j)'. Show the aggregation formula 'h_1' = σ(Σ_j α_1j W h_j)' and the updated node 'h_1'' (h_1 prime) as an output. Also indicate multi-head attention with 'K heads' annotation. Five neighbours, five attention coefficients. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.

name: WaveNet Dilated Causal Convolution Stack
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces the WaveNet dilated causal convolution figure. Title 'WaveNet: Dilated Causal Convolutions' at top in 32px bold. Draw FOUR stacked horizontal layers of nodes, bottom to top: 'Input' (dilation 1), 'Hidden Layer (dilation 2)', 'Hidden Layer (dilation 4)', 'Output (dilation 8)'. Each layer is a horizontal row of EXACTLY SIXTEEN small circular nodes evenly spaced and vertically aligned in columns across the four rows. Draw the dilated-causal connection edges: each node connects upward to nodes spaced by the layer's dilation factor (1, 2, 4, 8), only to PAST timesteps (causal: edges go up-and-to-the-right or straight up, never to a future/right-of-target input). The growing receptive field should be visible as the fan-out widening with depth. Label the dilation factor (1, 2, 4, 8) at the left of each layer. Sixteen nodes per row, four rows, dilations doubling. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.
requirements: All FOUR node rows (Input, three above) are fully visible with no clipping at any edge
Each row contains EXACTLY sixteen nodes, evenly spaced and column-aligned across rows
No two nodes overlap each other
Each layer's dilation factor (1, 2, 4, 8) is labelled at the left and legible without overlapping nodes
Connection edges respect causality: no edge connects a node to an input that is to its RIGHT (future timestep)

name: Squeeze-and-Excitation Residual Block
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces a Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) block embedded in a residual block. Title 'Squeeze-and-Excitation Residual Block' at top in 32px bold. Main path bottom to top: input feature map 'X (H x W x C)' -> a 'Residual Conv Block (F_tr)' -> feature map 'U (H x W x C)'. From U, the SE branch splits to the RIGHT: '1) Squeeze: Global Average Pool -> 1 x 1 x C' -> '2) Excitation: FC -> ReLU -> FC -> Sigmoid (1 x 1 x C)' producing a per-channel scale vector 's'. Then '3) Scale: U ⊙ s (channel-wise multiply)' recombines the SE scale with U via a '⊙' (element-wise multiply) node. Finally a residual skip-connection arrow carries X around the whole block to a '+' addition node producing the output. Annotate the reduction ratio 'r' in the excitation (C -> C/r -> C). The squeeze/excitation branch and the residual skip must be DISTINCT paths that do not cross through the main conv box. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.
requirements: All components (X input, Residual Conv Block, U, Squeeze GAP, Excitation FC-ReLU-FC-Sigmoid, Scale multiply ⊙, residual + node, output) are visible with no clipping at any edge
No two boxes overlap each other

name: Perceiver-IO Cross-Attention with Latent Array
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces the Perceiver-IO architecture figure. Title 'Perceiver IO' at top in 32px bold. LEFT: a tall 'Input Array (M x D, e.g. M = 50000)' box representing a large byte/pixel array. CENTER: a much smaller 'Latent Array (N x C, N = 512 << M)' box. The flow: (1) a 'Cross-Attention' box where the Latent Array is the QUERY and the Input Array provides KEYS and VALUES (draw arrows: latents -> Q, input array -> K and V) to encode the large input into the small latent; (2) a stack of 'Latent Transformer (Self-Attention) x L' blocks operating only on the latent array (show the 'L' multiplier); (3) an output 'Cross-Attention (Decode)' box where a separate 'Output Query Array (O x E)' attends into the processed latents to produce the 'Output Array'. Emphasize the asymmetric sizes (input large, latent small) with explicit M, N annotations. Show that self-attention cost is O(N²) on latents, not O(M²). HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.
requirements: All components (Input Array, Latent Array, encode Cross-Attention, Latent Transformer stack, Output Query Array, decode Cross-Attention, Output Array) are visible with no clipping at any edge
No two boxes overlap each other
Every box label and dimension annotation is fully contained inside its box with no overflow
The Input Array box is drawn visibly larger/taller than the Latent Array box, reflecting M >> N
In the encode Cross-Attention, the Latent Array connects as the QUERY and the Input Array connects as KEYS and VALUES, with these roles labelled (Q from latents, K/V from input)
The Latent Transformer self-attention stack shows an 'x L' (or 'L×') multiplier and operates on the latent array only
The decode stage shows a separate Output Query Array attending into the latents to produce the Output Array
Dimension annotations M, N, and their relation (N << M) are present and legible without colliding
Cross-attention K/V/Q arrows connect the correct boxes and do not pass through unrelated boxes

name: Evoformer (AlphaFold MSA + Pair Representation)
description: Create a single 1920x1080 self-contained HTML file using ONLY inline CSS and inline SVG that reproduces an AlphaFold-style Evoformer block figure. Title 'Evoformer Block (x48)' at top in 32px bold with the '48 blocks' multiplier shown. The block operates on TWO representations side by side: a LEFT 'MSA Representation (s x r x c_m)' drawn as a stacked grid (rows = sequences, columns = residues) and a RIGHT 'Pair Representation (r x r x c_z)' drawn as a square residue-by-residue grid. MSA-side sub-modules stacked vertically: 'MSA Row-wise Gated Self-Attention (with pair bias)', 'MSA Column-wise Gated Self-Attention', 'MSA Transition'. Pair-side sub-modules: 'Outer Product Mean' (arrow FROM MSA INTO the pair representation), 'Triangle Multiplicative Update (outgoing)', 'Triangle Multiplicative Update (incoming)', 'Triangle Self-Attention (starting node)', 'Triangle Self-Attention (ending node)', 'Pair Transition'. Draw the 'pair bias' arrow FROM the pair representation INTO the MSA row attention, and the 'Outer Product Mean' arrow FROM the MSA INTO the pair representation, making the two representations communicate bidirectionally. Show the '(x48)' block repetition. HARDEN (density + alignment): ALSO include (a) a LEGEND box that maps every distinct block color and every arrow/line style used in the figure, and (b) a small tensor-shape annotation on each major block (e.g. 'BxNxd'). Pack the figure densely with at least 14 labeled elements; every group of sibling blocks must be equal-size and share edges, with uniform gutters, and nothing may overlap or clip.

name: Vision Transformer (ViT)

name: Flamingo vision-language (gated cross-attention)

Mark frozen modules with a snowflake and trainable ones with a flame, explained in a legend.
